title: Investorer: Her er tre C25-aktier med større potentiale
summary_md:
# Investorer: Her er tre C25-aktier med større potentiale

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-05-20  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode centres on a revival of Danish IPOs (notably fish-feed group Biomar at a ~10bn DKK market cap and the upcoming Installatørgruppen) and on two analyst-favoured C25 themes: green-energy turnarounds (Ørsted, Vestas, NKT) and banks (Danske Bank, Sydbank, Jyske) benefiting from higher-for-longer rates. Hosts stress portfolio robustness, diversification and rebalancing amid inflation/rate risk, favouring value over richly-priced AI/chip and growth names like Tesla. Portfolio review highlights big winners Ericsson and DSV alongside laggards Chemometec and GN Store Nord.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 ORSTED — Ørsted | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Owned turnaround on US offshore wind; expectations were rock-bottom after the capital raise, now delivering on guidance and getting repriced.

#### Reasoning

Johnny owns it and sees more upside. The case is a turnaround where sentiment was crushed; Ørsted is simply delivering what it promised, driving a repricing. European energy-independence policy and reduced Chinese competition in wind are structural tailwinds. Higher rates are the main risk, raising financing costs and making risk-free bonds competitive with wind-project returns.

#### Sell Triggers

- if long-term rates rise sharply, raising project financing costs
- if US offshore wind policy reverses under Trump
- if execution slips below guidance again

> *"Örsted var verkligen en turnaround case på grund av havvind i USA... Så därför får vi en repricing av Örsted."*

---

### 🟢 VWS — Vestas | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Owned; among the best C25 performers with analysts lifting targets; expected to surprise positively, holding back upgrades only due to geopolitics.

#### Reasoning

Johnny owns Vestas and specifically expects it to surprise positively, noting it looks close to an upward guidance revision but is waiting due to geopolitical uncertainty. European energy-independence drive and reduced Chinese competition (after solar dumping) support pricing power and demand in coming years. Higher rates are the offsetting risk.

#### Sell Triggers

- if higher-for-longer rates pressure renewable valuations
- if expected positive guidance revision fails to materialise
- if Chinese competition intensifies in wind

> *"Speciellt Vestas tror jag kommer till att överraskas positivt. Det luktar ju också långt ifrån att de är så tätt på att uppjustera."*

---

### 🟢 NKT — NKT | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

European electrification structural play; analyst targets raised; lower risk than Ørsted but somewhat higher valuation.

#### Reasoning

Jesper calls NKT a sensible long-term structural trend on European electrification, with much lower risk than Ørsted. The main reservation is a richer valuation. Indirectly exposed to rising rates but the structural demand story dominates the thesis.

#### Sell Triggers

- if valuation expands further without earnings support
- if rising rates curb electrification investment
- if order intake slows

> *"Det enkla caset, NKT, det är ju elektrificering i Europa... Jag syns det är riktigt förnuftigt. Med mycket mindre risiko än i Örsted."*

---

### 🟢 DANSKE — Danske Bank | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 75%

Top portfolio holding, best performer over its lifetime; higher rates lift net interest income, gaining asset-mgmt share, ambitious new payout targets.

#### Reasoning

Jesper owns it and added more; the best-performing position. Higher-for-longer rates boost net interest income, while asset-light asset-management share gains and AI cost savings add upside. New financial targets cut the cost ratio toward <43%, free up capital (CET1 17.3% vs 16% target) and raise payout ambition to 60-70% with ~55bn in buybacks/dividends 2026-29. Analysts still see double-digit upside. Macro recession is the key risk.

#### Sell Triggers

- if rates rise high enough to trigger recession and credit losses
- if loan demand/credit quality deteriorates
- if bank fails to deliver on new capital-return targets

> *"deras mål det är att deras kärnkapital procent i banken den ska vara 16. Den är nu 17,3. Så det är faktiskt plats till att de kan gå ned i kapitaltrappen."*

---

### 🟡 JYSK — Jyske Bank | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Owned bank held at current weight; benefits from higher rates and a de-risked balance sheet after moving riskiest loans off-book.

#### Reasoning

Johnny holds both Danske and Jyske at current weight, seeing banks benefit from the current rate environment and a healthy economy. Even with 2-3 ECB hikes, rates stay historically low, supporting margins without choking the economy. Risk has shifted as banks moved the riskiest lending off balance sheet (to private debt). Fairly priced — neither cheap nor expensive.

#### Sell Triggers

- if economy rolls over and lending contracts
- if rates rise enough to hurt credit quality
- if banks become too conservative, limiting earnings growth

> *"Vi har både dansk bank och jysk bank. Och vi håller den vägt vi har nu i dem."*

---

### ⛔ TSLA — Tesla | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

P/E ~208 with cash flow far in the future; growth stocks like this hit hardest by rising discount rates in a higher-for-longer scenario.

#### Reasoning

Used as the archetype of an expensive growth stock vulnerable to higher-for-longer rates: with P/E ~208 its cash flows lie far in the future, so a higher discount factor hits it disproportionately versus a value name like Biomar (P/E 12). Hosts advise avoiding the most extreme high-multiple 'moon-rocket' names.

#### Sell Triggers

- if long-term rates stay elevated or rise further
- if growth/earnings disappoint relative to lofty multiple

> *"Tesla handlar på en price earning på 208 och så 12 i Biomar... i Tesla ska du ha en stjärnekikert för att se att det cashflowet ligger långt ut i framtiden."*

---

### 🟢 ERIC-B — Ericsson | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 80%

Best portfolio performer (+32%); bought ~140 now >300, Huawei barred from Europe creating a moat, improving capital structure, 5G rollout, sensible valuation.

#### Reasoning

Jesper calls it simply too good to be left out. Multiple positive triggers: Huawei excluded from Europe builds a protective moat, the ~50bn cash pile is being deployed via improved capital structure, organic growth and 5G rollout underpin earnings, and it should catch up to AI-driven Nokia given a shared telco-operator customer base. Low rate sensitivity, sensible valuation, and successful dividends. Strong conviction hold/buy.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Huawei regains European market access
- if telco operator capex/5G spending slows
- if capital-allocation discipline weakens

> *"Den är simpelthen för god till att den inte ska vara med. Så köpte vi den in omkring 140. Och nu ligger den ju gott över 300."*

---

### 🟢 DSV — DSV | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 75%

Owned (-6.7% YTD) but seen as mispriced; aggressive new capital-markets-day targets, ~9bn DKK DB Schenker synergies, clear AI winner.

#### Reasoning

Johnny finds it incomprehensible the stock sits where it does. Management's capital-markets-day targets (45% conversion ratio, >20% pre-tax RoIC, 6bn cost-out via AI) were more aggressive than expected and described as figures they are highly confident of hitting, implying further upside. ~9bn in DB Schenker synergies plus route optimisation. Seen as an AI winner, contrary to fears AI would disrupt transport; recently hit by Hormuz/geopolitical worries. Expects a clearly positive return this yea

#### Sell Triggers

- if DB Schenker synergies fail to materialise
- if freight/transport demand weakens further
- if Hormuz/geopolitical disruption persists

> *"Jag syns det är helt oförståeligt att den ligger här... För mig att se DSV är klart en AI-vinnare."*

---

### 🟢 IPCO — International Petroleum | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Energy holding added to for low market correlation and inflation hedge; energy best sector +35% YTD, dips on Hormuz resolution seen as entry points.

#### Reasoning

Jesper holds and added International Petroleum, valuing energy/mining for diversification and low correlation with the broader market — energy stocks fall on Hormuz de-escalation hopes while the rest rises, smoothing the portfolio. Also an inflation hedge if you believe in higher inflation. Not too late to add on geopolitical dips; everyone should hold some energy/commodities.

#### Sell Triggers

- if oil prices fall sharply on lasting Middle East peace
- if inflation expectations collapse
- if energy sector momentum reverses after +35% run

> *"man ska ha något energi och mining i sin portefölj. Det ger bara en god spredning. Också i förhållande till inflation."*

---

### 🔴 NOVO-B — Novo Nordisk | **SELL** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Neutral | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Used as the rebalancing example: investors overweight Novo should trim into strength and diversify rather than stay emotionally attached.

#### Reasoning

Not a fundamental call on Novo but a portfolio-discipline message: positions shouldn't exceed ~10% of a portfolio, so heavily overweight Novo holders should rebalance — sell some and buy boring banks or oil for diversification. Hosts recall trimming Novo on the way down for rebalancing; the lesson is to stay 'ice-cold' and unattached.

#### Sell Triggers

- if position size exceeds ~10% of portfolio (rebalance)
- if concentration risk rises versus diversification goals

> *"nu vet jag att många har mycket Novo till exempel. Då bör de ju rebalansera... Sälj något Novo, köp något annat."*

---

### 🟡 CHEMM — Chemometec | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Portfolio holding down 52% YTD; had been very expensive, now a long road back to recovery.

#### Reasoning

Mentioned as Peter Beckgård's worst contributor, down 52% year-to-date because the shares had been very expensive. No active buy/sell intent stated; framed as a still-held laggard with a long way home, implying continued holding rather than capitulation.

#### Sell Triggers

- if valuation remains stretched with no earnings recovery
- if fundamentals deteriorate further

> *"hans Chemometic-aktier de har varit riktigt dyra. De har kostat oss ett tap på 52 procent årets datum."*

---

### 🟠 SYDB — Sydbank | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Bank whose analyst price targets were raised alongside Danske Bank as part of the favoured banking theme.

#### Reasoning

Grouped into the second analyst-favoured theme (banks) where price targets have been lifted on higher rates, strong asset management and AI cost savings. Not a held position discussed in depth, so a watch on the same positive banking thesis driving Danske and Jyske.

#### Sell Triggers

- if rate environment reverses
- if credit losses rise in a downturn

> *"Både Danske Bank och AL Sudbank har fått skruva upp för deras kursmål."*

---
